task: Launch-quality advanced task: Catastrophe Model Vulnerability Curve Challenge. Reality context: an insurer is validating vulnerability curves after a severe convective storm season. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=hazard_intensity, metric2=modeled_loss_ratio, metric3=observed_loss_ratio, metric4=credibility_weight; records: R1 hazard_intensity=87.65 modeled_loss_ratio=841408 observed_loss_ratio=27917 credibility_weight=78.67; R2 hazard_intensity=32.58 modeled_loss_ratio=827974 observed_loss_ratio=358005 credibility_weight=80.75; R3 hazard_intensity=54.64 modeled_loss_ratio=103634 observed_loss_ratio=625983 credibility_weight=93.73; R4 hazard_intensity=15.91 modeled_loss_ratio=108720 observed_loss_ratio=509677 credibility_weight=47.17; limit_hazard_intensity=79.11; limit_modeled_loss_ratio=204527; limit_observed_loss_ratio=536845; decision_budget_usd=70000; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="certification workflow expected reproducible calculations and signoff rationale". Rules and constraints: Use worst-case scoring: normalize each metric to the embedded maximum, apply weights 0.40, 0.30, 0.20, 0.10, and classify the top quartile as immediate action. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: calibrate curve and flag bias; calculate derived metrics, classify every record, identify the smallest defensible intervention, and write the decision rationale. Deliver cat_vulnerability_review.xlsx + actuarial_memo.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
